title: Trie Data Structure
prompt:
Write a Hemlock program implementing a Trie (prefix tree) for string storage and lookup. Implement:

1. `trie_new()` - creates a new trie node: {children: {}, is_end: false}
2. `trie_insert(root, word)` - inserts a word character by character
3. `trie_search(root, word)` - returns true if the exact word exists
4. `trie_starts_with(root, prefix)` - returns true if any word starts with prefix
5. `trie_words_with_prefix(root, prefix)` - returns array of all words matching prefix (sorted)

Use object properties for children mapping (char -> child node). Use the `chars()` string method or `char_at()` to iterate characters.

Test:
- Insert: "apple", "app", "application", "apply", "apt", "bat", "batch", "bath"
- search("app"): print "search app: true"
- search("ap"): print "search ap: false"
- starts_with("app"): print "prefix app: true"
- starts_with("baz"): print "prefix baz: false"
- words_with_prefix("app"): print "words with app: app, apple, application, apply"
- words_with_prefix("bat"): print "words with bat: bat, batch, bath"
expected_output:
search app: true
search ap: false
prefix app: true
prefix baz: false
words with app: app, apple, application, apply
words with bat: bat, batch, bath
